g.text: Human Secure Socket Host — Consent-Driven Personal Intelligence. Your data. Your agents. Yours to own.
Read down: ① decode the words · ② the platform (what it is) · ③ end-to-end flows (how every story connects). [LIGHT MODE]
— every acronym in plain English (decode FIRST, then read the map)
Personal Knowledge Model — your encrypted memory
Bring Your Own Key — only you hold it
Model Context Protocol — the AI tool bridge
— what it is. Seven layers; each card shows the name, its generic role (tag), then what · why · how · e.g. Read top → down.
— where a person or an AI meets Hussh — web, native, on-device, external hosts
browser app on shared React shell
the surfaces reach people through governed channels …
— governed ways results & capabilities reach users, devs, partners
an AI host asks for data
channels distribute the same governed truth to the agents that …
— reason, debate, delegate & act inside scoped authority — never raw keys
act on scoped memory provided by …
— the heart: one encrypted store the user truly owns (zero-knowledge)
the encrypted PKM, whose every read/write is gated by …
— every action proves identity & earns consent first — the gate
the trust & consent layer, enforced through …
— the backend that enforces policy & brings chosen data in
core platform services, all running on the infrastructure foundation.
— the governed foundation everything above runs on
— how it connects. Each story is ONE left → right sequence of real steps. Lanes never cross.
CRM updated — never a PKM mirror
Greenfield (future): AI-memory import · public-profile→PKM · broker execution · partner-system sync · on-device edge.
Grounded in the canonical Hussh platform docs, the SDK protocol spec, and the research engine. Status is honest, not aspirational.
